info.name: WorkForcePro – API Test Suite
info.description:
Complete Postman test collection for WorkForcePro Workforce Management Platform.

Covers all 7 endpoints across Employees and Training APIs with positive, negative, and edge-case scenarios.

SETUP BEFORE RUNNING:
1. Set {{base_url}}    → e.g. https://api.workforcepro.com
2. Set {{auth_token}}  → valid Supervisor-level Bearer token
3. Set {{training_id}} → ID of an existing training module (e.g. TRN-001)
4. Run in ORDER — PS-001 auto-populates {{employee_id}} for all downstream requests.

exec: pm.test('[PS-001] Status code is 201 Created', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(201); });
pm.test('[PS-001] Response time under 2000ms', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.responseTime).to.be.below(2000); });
pm.test('[PS-001] Content-Type is application/json', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.headers.get('Content-Type')).to.include('application/json'); });
pm.test('[PS-001] Response body is valid JSON', () => { pm.response.to.be.json; });
const json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('[PS-001] Response contains required fields: id, employee_id, name, email, department', () => {
['id','employee_id','name','email','department','supervisor_id'].forEach(f => pm.expect(json, 'Missing: '+f).to.have.property(f));
pm.test('[PS-001] Returned name matches submitted name', () => { pm.expect(json.name).to.equal('Alice Johnson'); });
pm.test('[PS-001] Returned email matches submitted email (case-normalised)', () => { pm.expect(json.email.toLowerCase()).to.equal('alice.johnson@workforcepro.com'); });
pm.test('[PS-001] Email format is valid', () => { pm.expect(json.email).to.match(/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/); });
pm.test('[PS-001] id field is non-empty', () => { pm.expect(json.id).to.exist; pm.expect(String(json.id).length).to.be.above(0); });
pm.test('[PS-001] employee_id matches submitted value', () => { pm.expect(json.employee_id).to.equal('EMP-3001'); });
if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('employee_id', String(json.id)); console.log('[PS-001] Saved employee_id:', json.id); }
pm.test('[PS-002] Status code is 200 OK', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
pm.test('[PS-002] Response time under 1000ms', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.responseTime).to.be.below(1000); });
pm.test('[PS-002] Response is valid JSON', () => { pm.response.to.be.json; });
const json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('[PS-002] Response contains all employee fields', () => { ['id','employee_id','name','email','department'].forEach(f => pm.expect(json,'Missing: '+f).to.have.property(f)); });
pm.test('[PS-002] Returned id matches requested id', () => { pm.expect(String(json.id)).to.equal(pm.collectionVariables.get('employee_id')); });
pm.test('[PS-002] Name is non-empty string', () => { pm.expect(json.name).to.be.a('string').and.not.be.empty; });
pm.test('[PS-002] Email is valid format', () => { pm.expect(json.email).to.match(/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/); });
pm.test('[PS-003] Status code is 200 OK', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
const json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('[PS-003] Updated department reflected in response', () => { pm.expect(json.department).to.equal('Operations'); });
pm.test('[PS-003] Updated supervisor_id reflected in response', () => { pm.expect(json.supervisor_id).to.equal('SUP-002'); });
pm.test('[PS-003] id is unchanged after update', () => { pm.expect(String(json.id)).to.equal(pm.collectionVariables.get('employee_id')); });
pm.test('[PS-003] updated_at timestamp present', () => { pm.expect(json).to.have.property('updated_at'); pm.expect(json.updated_at).to.not.be.null; });
pm.test('[PS-004] Status code is 201 Created', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(201); });
const json = pm.response.json();

pm.test('[PS-004] Response contains assignment fields: id, employee_id, training_id, status, due_date', () => { ['id','employee_id','training_id','status','due_date'].forEach(f => pm.expect(json,'Missing: '+f).to.have.property(f)); });
pm.test('[PS-004] Status is Pending on new assignment', () => { pm.expect(json.status.toLowerCase()).to.be.oneOf(['pending','assigned']); });
pm.test('[PS-004] due_date is present and non-empty', () => { pm.expect(json.due_date).to.exist.and.not.be.empty; });
pm.test('[PS-004] due_date is valid ISO 8601 date', () => { pm.expect(json.due_date).to.match(/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}/); });
pm.test('[PS-004] employee_id matches request', () => { pm.expect(String(json.employee_id)).to.equal(pm.collectionVariables.get('employee_id')); });
if (json.id) { pm.collectionVariables.set('assignment_id', String(json.id)); }
pm.test('[PS-005] Status code is 200 OK', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
const json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('[PS-005] Status is awaiting_approval after completion', () => { pm.expect(json.status.toLowerCase()).to.be.oneOf(['awaiting_approval','pending_approval','completed']); });
pm.test('[PS-005] completion_date is present and non-null', () => { pm.expect(json.completion_date).to.exist.and.not.be.null; });
pm.test('[PS-005] BUSINESS RULE: completion_date is not a future date', () => {
const cd = new Date(json.completion_date); const today = new Date(); today.setHours(23,59,59,999);
pm.expect(cd.getTime()).to.be.at.most(today.getTime());
pm.test('[PS-005] notes field is present', () => { pm.expect(json).to.have.property('notes'); });
pm.test('[PS-006] Status code is 200 OK', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });
const json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('[PS-006] Status is approved', () => { pm.expect(json.status.toLowerCase()).to.equal('approved'); });
pm.test('[PS-006] approved_by is present and non-empty', () => { pm.expect(json).to.have.property('approved_by'); pm.expect(String(json.approved_by)).to.not.be.empty; });
pm.test('[PS-006] approved_at timestamp is present', () => { pm.expect(json).to.have.property('approved_at'); pm.expect(json.approved_at).to.not.be.null; });
pm.test('[PS-006] employee_id matches request', () => { pm.expect(String(json.employee_id)).to.equal(pm.collectionVariables.get('employee_id')); });
pm.test('[PS-007] Status code is 200 or 204', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([200,204]); });
pm.test('[PS-007] Response time under 2000ms', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.responseTime).to.be.below(2000); });
if (pm.response.code === 200) { pm.test('[PS-007] Response confirms deletion', () => { const j = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(j.message || j.deleted || j.success).to.exist; }); }
pm.test('[NS-001] Status is 409 or 422 – duplicate email rejected', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([409,422]); });
const json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('[NS-001] Error response contains error or message field', () => { pm.expect(json.error || json.message || json.errors).to.exist; });

pm.test('[NS-001] Error message references email or duplicate', () => { const t = JSON.stringify(json).toLowerCase(); pm.expect(t).to.satisfy(s => s.includes('email') || s.includes('duplicate') || s.includes('already') || s.includes('exists')); });
pm.test('[NS-001] No new employee record returned (no id)', () => { pm.expect(json.id).to.not.exist; });
if (pm.response.code===200) { console.error('[NS-001] DEFECT BUG-001: Duplicate email accepted – second record created'); }
pm.test('[NS-002] Status code is 404 Not Found', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(404); });
const json = pm.response.json();
pm.test('[NS-002] Error message is present', () => { pm.expect(json.error || json.message || json.detail).to.exist; });

pm.test('[NS-002] No stack trace or DB error exposed', () => { const b = pm.response.text().toLowerCase(); ['stacktrace','stack_trace','activerecord','sqlexception','ora-','mysql'].forEach(l => pm.expect(b,'Leaked: '+l).to.not.include(l)); });

item.name: NS-003 | POST /api/employees – Missing Required Fields Rejected

exec: pm.test('[NS-003] Status is 400 or 422 for missing fields', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([400,422]); });
const json = pm.response.json();

pm.test('[NS-003] Error references required or missing fields', () => { const t = JSON.stringify(json).toLowerCase(); pm.expect(t).to.satisfy(s => s.includes('required') || s.includes('missing') || s.includes('blank') || s.includes('name')); });
pm.test('[NS-003] No employee record created', () => { pm.expect(json.id).to.not.exist; });

item.name: NS-004 | POST /api/trainings/assign – Invalid Training ID Rejected

exec: pm.test('[NS-004] Status is 404 or 422 for invalid training_id', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([404,422]); });
const json = pm.response.json();

pm.test('[NS-004] Error references training or not found', () => { const t = JSON.stringify(json).toLowerCase(); pm.expect(t).to.satisfy(s => s.includes('training') || s.includes('not found') || s.includes('invalid')); });
pm.test('[NS-004] No assignment created', () => { pm.expect(json.id).to.not.exist; });
pm.test('[NS-005] Status is 403, 404, or 422 – unassigned training blocked', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([403,404,422]); });
const json = pm.response.json();

pm.test('[NS-005] Error references not assigned or not found', () => { const t = JSON.stringify(json).toLowerCase(); pm.expect(t).to.satisfy(s => s.includes('not assigned') || s.includes('not found') || s.includes('forbidden') || s.includes('assigned')); });
pm.test('[NS-005] Training NOT marked as completed', () => { pm.expect(json.status && json.status.toLowerCase()==='completed').to.be.false; });

item.name: NS-006 | POST /api/trainings/approve – Approve Non-Completed Training Blocked (OJT-003)

exec: pm.test('[NS-006] Status is 400, 403, or 422 – pending training cannot be approved', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([400,403,422]); });
const json = pm.response.json();

pm.test('[NS-006] Error references status or completion requirement', () => { const t = JSON.stringify(json).toLowerCase(); pm.expect(t).to.satisfy(s => s.includes('not completed') || s.includes('pending') || s.includes('status') || s.includes('cannot approve')); });
pm.test('[NS-006] Status is NOT approved', () => { pm.expect(json.status && json.status.toLowerCase()==='approved').to.be.false; });
if (pm.response.code===200) { console.error('[NS-006] DEFECT: Pending training approved without completion – OJT-003 not enforced at API level'); }

item.name: NS-007 | POST /api/trainings/complete – Future Completion Date Rejected

exec: pm.test('[NS-007] Status is 400 or 422 – future completion date rejected', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([400,422]); });
const json = pm.response.json();

pm.test('[NS-007] Error references invalid date or future', () => { const t = JSON.stringify(json).toLowerCase(); pm.expect(t).to.satisfy(s => s.includes('date') || s.includes('future') || s.includes('invalid')); });
if (pm.response.code===200) { console.error('[NS-007] DEFECT BUG-005 CONFIRMED: Future completion date accepted by API'); }

item.name: NS-008 | Any Endpoint – Unauthenticated Request Returns 401

exec: pm.test('[NS-008] Status code is 401 Unauthorized', () => { pm.response.to.have.status(401); });
pm.test('[NS-008] Protected data not exposed in response', () => { const b = pm.response.text(); pm.expect(b).to.not.include('@workforcepro.com'); pm.expect(b).to.not.include('employee_id'); });

pm.test('[NS-008] Error indicates authentication required', () => { const t = pm.response.text().toLowerCase(); pm.expect(t).to.satisfy(s => s.includes('unauthorized') || s.includes('unauthenticated') || s.includes('token') || s.includes('auth')); });

item.name: NS-009 | PUT /api/employees/{id} – Update to Duplicate Email Rejected

exec: pm.test('[NS-009] Status is 409 or 422 – duplicate email on update rejected', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([409,422]); });
const json = pm.response.json();

pm.test('[NS-009] Error references email uniqueness', () => { const t = JSON.stringify(json).toLowerCase(); pm.expect(t).to.satisfy(s => s.includes('email') || s.includes('duplicate') || s.includes('taken') || s.includes('exists')); });
if (pm.response.code===200) { console.error('[NS-009] DEFECT: Duplicate email accepted on PUT – variant of BUG-001'); }

item.name: NS-010 | POST /api/trainings/approve – Reject with Blank Reason Blocked

exec: pm.test('[NS-010] Status is 400 or 422 – blank rejection_reason rejected', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([400,422]); });
const json = pm.response.json();

pm.test('[NS-010] Error references rejection_reason being required', () => { const t = JSON.stringify(json).toLowerCase(); pm.expect(t).to.satisfy(s => s.includes('reason') || s.includes('required') || s.includes('blank')); });
if (pm.response.code===200) { console.error('[NS-010] DEFECT BUG-006 CONFIRMED: Empty rejection_reason accepted at API level'); }

item.name: EC-001 | POST /api/employees – SQL Injection in Name Field Blocked

exec: pm.test('[EC-001] Status is 400 or 422 – SQL injection payload rejected', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([400,422]); });

pm.test('[EC-001] No DB error or SQL syntax exposed', () => { const b = pm.response.text().toLowerCase(); ['syntax error','sql','mysql','postgresql','ora-','sqlite'].forEach(l => pm.expect(b,'DB info leaked: '+l).to.not.include(l)); });

item.name: EC-002 | POST /api/employees – Empty Body {} Handled Gracefully

exec: pm.test('[EC-002] Status is 400 or 422 – empty body rejected', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.be.oneOf([400,422]); });
pm.test('[EC-002] No 500 Internal Server Error – empty body handled gracefully', () => { pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.equal(500); });

item.name: EC-003 | POST /api/trainings/assign – Past Due Date Boundary Check

exec: pm.test('[EC-003] Status noted – past due_date should be rejected or flagged with warning', () => {
const code = pm.response.code;
console.log('[EC-003] Actual status:', code, '| Expected: 400/422 to reject, or 201 with overdue flag');
pm.expect(code).to.be.oneOf([201,400,422]);

if (pm.response.code===201) { const json = pm.response.json(); pm.test('[EC-003] If accepted: overdue warning flag present', () => { pm.expect(json.warning || json.overdue || (json.status && json.status.toLowerCase().includes('overdue')),'Past due date silently accepted – compliance risk').to.exist; }); }
